plain_language_summary: A1c reflects your average blood sugar over about the past 2–3 months.
why_it_matters: It helps monitor diabetes and long-term blood sugar control.
common_reasons_high: Diabetes or prediabetes; certain conditions can also affect results.
common_reasons_low: May be seen with conditions that shorten red blood cell lifespan.
when_to_contact_clinician: Contact your clinician if levels are persistently above target or if you have symptoms of high/low blood sugar.

plain_language_summary: Glucose, fasting is a common lab test. Results are interpreted in the context of your symptoms and other labs.
why_it_matters: It can help monitor overall health and specific organs depending on the test.
common_reasons_high: May reflect many causes including dehydration, diet, medications, or organ function changes.
common_reasons_low: May reflect nutritional status, hydration, or other conditions depending on the test.
when_to_contact_clinician: Contact your clinician if you have concerning symptoms or repeated abnormal results.

common_reasons_low: May reflect nutritional status, hydration, or other conditions depending on the test.

plain_language_summary: Creatinine is commonly used to assess kidney function.
why_it_matters: Kidneys filter waste; changes can reflect hydration, medications, or kidney disease.
common_reasons_high: Dehydration, certain medications, or kidney function changes.
common_reasons_low: Often not concerning; can relate to low muscle mass or other factors.
when_to_contact_clinician: Seek care promptly if you have reduced urination, swelling, severe weakness, or rapid worsening values.

plain_language_summary: A lipid panel measures different fats in your blood (LDL, HDL, and triglycerides).
why_it_matters: It helps estimate heart and blood vessel risk and guides lifestyle/medication decisions.
common_reasons_high: Dietary patterns, genetics, diabetes, thyroid issues, and other factors.
common_reasons_low: Very low values are uncommon; interpretation depends on the specific test.
when_to_contact_clinician: Discuss results with your clinician, especially if LDL or triglycerides are high.

plain_language_summary: TSH helps evaluate thyroid function.
why_it_matters: Thyroid hormones affect energy, heart rate, and metabolism.
common_reasons_high: May suggest underactive thyroid depending on other thyroid labs and symptoms.
common_reasons_low: May suggest overactive thyroid depending on other thyroid labs and symptoms.
when_to_contact_clinician: Contact your clinician if you have palpitations, unexplained weight changes, severe fatigue, or abnormal results.

common_reasons_low: May suggest overactive thyroid depending on other thyroid labs and symptoms.
when_to_contact_clinician: Contact your clinician if you have palpitations, unexplained weight changes, severe fatigue, or abnormal results.

plain_language_summary: These are common parts of a complete blood count (CBC).
why_it_matters: CBC helps assess infection/inflammation, anemia, and clotting-related conditions.
common_reasons_high: Infection, inflammation, stress, or other causes depending on the component.
common_reasons_low: Anemia or low cell counts can have many causes and need clinician interpretation.
when_to_contact_clinician: Seek care if you have severe bleeding, shortness of breath, fainting, or persistent fever.

plain_language_summary: Urinalysis checks for substances in urine such as protein or glucose.
why_it_matters: It can help screen for kidney issues, diabetes, and urinary tract problems.
common_reasons_high: Protein/glucose can appear with kidney disease, diabetes, or temporary conditions.
common_reasons_low: Negative is typically expected for these screening items.
when_to_contact_clinician: Contact your clinician if the result is positive or you have pain with urination, fever, or blood in urine.
